🔍

How This Works

Yes, someone actually built this for you
🌴What Is This Thing
This is your group's personal Key West command center. Eight adults. Four nights. One app. What could go wrong.

It has your flights, the weather, a shared calendar, local recommendations, a photo gallery, and a button that finds the nearest bar. It also has a button that sends Chris home. We'll get to that.
👤Who Are You
The first time you open the app you'll see a screen asking who you are. Tap your drink emoji. The app remembers you forever after that — your name shows up in the greeting, your check-ins are tagged to you, and your Do Not Push count is yours.

If you picked the wrong person — it happens, especially after a few drinks — tap Not you? under your name on the home screen and pick again.

Your drink emoji: Christy 🍷 · Bill 🥃 · Leighann 🧉 · Mike 🍻 · Lisa 🍺 · Chris 🫗 · Rachel 🍹 · Andrew 🧃
📲Add This To Your Home Screen
Do this now. Before you forget. Before the rum.

On iPhone (Safari only — won't work in Chrome):
1. Make sure you're viewing this in Safari. If you opened it in Chrome, copy the link and paste it into Safari.
2. Tap the Share button at the bottom of the screen — it looks like a box with an arrow pointing up ⬆️
3. Scroll down in the menu that pops up until you see "Add to Home Screen"
4. Tap it. Give it a name if you want. Tap Add in the top right corner.
5. Done. It now lives on your home screen like a real app.

On Android (Andrew):
1. Tap the three dots ⋮ in the top right corner of Chrome
2. Tap "Add to Home screen" or "Install app"
3. Tap Add. Done.
4. If it doesn't work, that's on Android, not us.

If you can't figure it out, ask Andrew. He brought a drone to a beach vacation, he can handle this.
📅The Calendar
All trip events live here — group events and couple events. Tap any event to get walking directions from wherever you're standing right now.

Tap the ✏️ icon on any event to edit the details or delete it entirely. Tap + Add Event at the bottom to add something new.

The home screen shows today's schedule automatically — no need to dig into the calendar just to know what's happening.
📍Where Is Everyone
When you go somewhere, tap Check In My Location and enter where you are. Takes five seconds.

Each person has a 💬 text and 📞 call button next to their name — tap either to reach them directly without leaving the app.

When someone checks in, everyone in the group gets an automatic text notification. No need to be watching the app — it comes to you.

⚠️ Important: Checking in at Sloppy Joe's at 4pm and then going dark does not mean you are still at Sloppy Joe's at midnight. It means you were at Sloppy Joe's at 4pm. Nobody knows where you are now. You could be at The Roost. You could be at The Saint. You could be face down in an alley off Duval Street serenading a stray cat.

Chris. We are looking at you. Update your check-in. Lisa found you once. She divorced you once too. Don't push it.
🆘I Need...
Your lifeline tab. Eight buttons covering every vacation emergency:

🍺 I'm Thirsty — nearest 3 bars with directions
🍔 I'm Hungry — nearest 3 restaurants
☕ Need Coffee — nearest 3 cafes
🏖️ Beach Me — nearest beach access
🛍️ I Want to Shop — nearest shops
🛏️ I Need Sleep — directions back to The Reach. No thinking required. Designed for use after midnight on Duval Street when thinking is no longer an option.
🍋 I Need Key Lime Pie — the definitive guide to 9 spots ranked by local consensus
🦪 I Need Oysters — 6 spots ranked by Reddit, with honest notes

🚨 I Need Help — genuine emergencies only. Hospital, pharmacy, 911, hotel phone. We hope you never need it. We're also realists about four nights on Duval Street.
📝Group Notes
A shared bulletin board for the whole group. Tap Group Notes on the home screen, type something (280 characters max), and tap Post Note. It shows up for everyone instantly.

Use it for the stuff that doesn't fit anywhere else — "left my sunglasses at the bar," "dinner reservation moved to 8," "does anyone have Dramamine." Your own notes get a small ✕ so you can delete them later. Everyone else's are safe from you.
🎯Trivia
Ten categories, 25 questions each, multiple choice. Pick a category from the home screen, answer away. You get two attempts per question — get it right and it's locked in as correct, miss twice and it's locked in as a loss. Either way that question is done; no replaying it to farm points.

Categories include Florida Keys, Kentucky Bourbon, decade-by-decade music from the 70s through 90s, Movies 1980-2010, MTV Before Reality Shows, Famous Movie Quotes, Is It a KY County, and What Did It Cost in 1993.

There's a shared leaderboard at the top showing everyone's cumulative correct answers across every category. Top three get medals. Bragging rights are permanent and will be referenced for years.
📸Scavenger Hunt
14 categories, photo-based, running the whole trip. Pick a category — rooster sightings, the Christy nap watch, key lime pie selfies, all 14 — and submit a photo using the camera or your camera roll. Up to 2 photos per person per category, so make them count.

Voting stays locked until Saturday at 4:00 PM — by then everyone's done submitting and nobody can vote on an incomplete field. Once it opens, tap into any category to see every submission and vote for your favorite. Vote counts are visible to everyone in real time. Once you vote, that's it — no changing your mind.

Tap 🏆 View All Results from the category list to see the current winner in every category at a glance, updating live as votes come in. Everyone gets a text the moment voting opens — good timing for the trip home.
🚨Incident Reports
The yellow button on the home screen tracks hours since the last middle-aged or alcohol-related incident, factory-safety-sign style. It starts at zero the moment the trip begins and counts up from there — until someone needs to file a report.

Tap Report an Injury to open the intake form: who was injured, what happened, whether it was self-inflicted or someone else's fault, witnesses, and whether you require legal representation. Every report resets the counter for the whole group, no exceptions, no judgment calls about what counts.

Say yes to legal representation and our in-house counsel will be formally notified. Frivolous claims are not just tolerated, they're encouraged — every case filed is one billable hour closer to funding next year's trip for certain people we won't name here.

Changed your mind mid-report? Tap the ✕ Cancel button in the top corner at any point and nothing gets saved.
🧾The Tab
Group expense tracking. Whoever fronts the money for dinner, the boat, the rum tour, whatever — log it here and the app figures out who owes who.

Tap + Log an Expense, enter what it was and the whole-dollar amount, pick who paid, then choose how to split it:

Split Evenly — divides the total across all 8 people, including the payer if they were part of it
Exclude Someone — same even split, just leave out whoever didn't take part
Custom $ — type an exact whole-dollar amount per person. The app won't let you save until the amounts add up to exactly the total — there's a live "remaining" indicator to help you get there.

The balance strip at the top shows everyone's overall position at a glance. The Settle Up tab is where the real detail lives — it's organized by who's owed money, and under each person's name is exactly who owes them and how much, traced straight back to real expenses. No clever math collapsing things into "whoever owes the most pays whoever's owed the most." If Christy owes Bill $125 for dinner, that's the line you'll see. Tap Mark Paid once that specific debt is actually settled, and it zeroes out for good — the original expense stays in the list as the receipt, but the balance reflects reality.

You can only delete an expense you personally logged. Everyone else's is locked.
💡Local Recs
Over 50 curated spots across bars, breakfast, dinner, beaches, attractions and landmarks. Ratings pulled live from Google.

The 💬 teal notes are real local intel — we scraped Reddit's r/KeyWest and other social media so you get the unfiltered opinions of people who actually live there and visit regularly, not sponsored TripAdvisor content. When a local says Half Shell Raw Bar is the best oyster spot on the island, that means something. When they warn you about shell shards at Alonzo's, that also means something.

Use the search bar at the top to find anything instantly. Type "oysters," "breakfast," "cheap," "meringue," or "local" and matching spots appear immediately. No scrolling required.

Every listing is tappable for walking directions.
📸Photos
Take photos with your regular camera app like a normal person. Then come here and tap Upload Photos to share your favorites with the group.

Or tap Take Photo to shoot directly in the app. Quality is slightly lower but perfectly fine for "proof that Bill actually caught a fish" and "evidence of whatever Chris was doing at 1am."

At the end of the trip everyone has access to everyone else's photos. No more group texts with 47 attachments that half the group never downloads.

If you upload something you want to remove, tap the ✕ button on your own photo thumbnail to delete it. You can only delete photos you uploaded — everyone else's are safe from you.
✈️Flights
All four United flight legs are in here — SDF to ORD, ORD to EYW outbound on July 7th, and both legs home on July 11th.

The app is connected to live flight data and will automatically update flight status as the airline makes changes — delays, gate changes, cancellations. You don't need to do anything.

Important: This feature won't show accurate live data until approximately 2 weeks before departure — around June 23rd. Before that the status badges will be blank. That's normal. Once the flights appear in the system they'll update automatically.

Tap Live Flight Status to check United's site directly. Tap My Booking to manage your own reservation — your confirmation number is your business.
🗺️Directions
Almost everything in this app is tappable for directions. Calendar events, local recs, landmarks, I Need results — tap it and Google Maps opens with walking directions from wherever you're standing.

Key West is about two miles end to end. You are almost certainly closer than you think. Unless you are Chris, in which case your exact position is unknown and has been for the last three hours.
🚁A Note About the Drone
Andrew has a drone. Possibly two. Nobody is surprised.

Key West has FAA flight restrictions over parts of the island and near the airport. Before Andrew launches anything over Duval Street at sunset, someone should probably mention this. That someone is not going to be Chris, who will be busy.

On the bright side, if Chris does go missing, Andrew's drone has a better chance of finding him than any of us do on foot. Every group needs a drone guy. We've got ours.

📸 Aerial photos from Andrew's drone are welcome in the shared photo gallery.
🏖️Final Thought
This app exists so that eight adults who theoretically know how to travel can spend less time texting "where are you" and more time actually being in Key West together.

Use it. Check in when you wander off. Share your photos. Tap the sleep button when the time comes. Let Andrew fly his drone. Keep an eye on Chris.

Now put your phone down and go enjoy the island. 🌴
🤖A Note About Andrew
Andrew uses an Android.

The app works on Android. Mostly. We think. We haven't tested it as thoroughly as the seven iPhone users in the group because, frankly, his opinion counts slightly less in these matters.

If Andrew reports a bug, we will log it, consider it carefully, and then ask one of the iPhone users if they can reproduce it before doing anything about it.

Andrew, if you're reading this: we appreciate you. We appreciate your drone. We do not appreciate your life choices in mobile operating systems. Welcome aboard anyway. 🤖

Group Notes

Quick notes for the whole group to see

Trivia

10 categories. 2 chances per question.
🏆Leaderboard

Scavenger Hunt

14 categories. Submit all trip. Vote starts Sat 4PM.
Tap a category below, snap or upload a photo that fits it (up to 2 per person per category), and the whole group can see and vote on every submission once voting opens. Every category has its own little contest going — rooster sightings, the Christy nap watch, key lime pie selfies, all of it. There's no wrong way to play except not playing.

Voting opens Saturday at 4:00 PM — perfect airport timing. You'll get a text when it does. Once you vote in a category, that vote is locked, so pick wisely.

The Tab

$0 logged · everyone's even
Log group expenses here and the app figures out who owes who. Split evenly, leave someone out, or set exact dollar amounts per person. Settle up when it's time to pay people back.
🧾 Log an Expense
Who paid?
Split how?

Duval Street

The Reach to Mallory Square, south to north
A quick visual cheat sheet of what's where along Duval Street. Tap any stop for walking directions. Roughly south to north, top to bottom — everything here is close enough to hit several in one walk.
Pinch to zoom · Drag to pan · Tap ✕ to close

Incident Reports

Safety first. Allegedly.
Use this section to report any injuries sustained due to excessive alcohol consumption, middle age, or some combination of the two. Examples include but are not limited to: "I threw my back out while sneezing," "I busted up my knee when Chris fell on me," and "I'm not sure what happened but it's Bill's fault."

The counter above tracks how long the group has gone without an incident. Every report resets it to zero. There is no penalty for filing a report and no reward either, except the satisfaction of having it documented for posterity.

or Alcohol-Related Incident

The App Development Team

Do Not Push

You pushed it anyway. Of course you did.
Researchers have long studied the compulsion to push buttons labeled DO NOT PUSH. After extensive field research involving this group, scientists have concluded that Chris is the problem.

Science thanks you for your contribution to the data.

Scavenger Hunt Photo Management
Step 1 — choose a category:
Step 2 — choose a photo to delete:
Or wipe everything — all photos and all votes across every category:
Pin a Group Note
Pinned notes stay at the top of Group Notes for everyone — good for room numbers, that kind of thing.
Trip Recap
Assembles everything into one shareable recap page — photos, leaderboards, scavenger hunt winners, incident log, and group notes.
App Status
Functional prototype. All UI complete. Static data. Real weather via Open-Meteo API active. Firebase, Twilio, FlightAware, and Google Places pending credential setup.

Pending — To Go Live
🔴 Firebase Realtime DB — Check-ins persist across all 8 devices
🔴 Firebase Storage — Photos shared across group, survive session
🔴 Twilio SMS — Push notifications on check-in & weather alerts
     • A2P 10DLC registration required (~$4 one-time)
     • Phone number rental ~$1.15/mo
🔴 FlightAware AeroAPI — Live flight status (currently static)
🔴 Google Places API — Live ratings on recs (currently static)
🔴 GitHub Pages — Host at permanent URL to share with group
Estimated Trip Cost
Firebase: $0  |  Firebase Storage: ~$0.50
Twilio number: $1.15  |  A2P reg: $4.00 (one-time)
Twilio SMS (~300 msgs): ~$2.50
FlightAware: ~$1.50  |  Google Places: ~$2.00
GitHub Pages: $0  |  Open-Meteo: $0
Total estimated: ~$11.65
Known Issues / To-Do
• I Need → Bars still showing landmarks on cached versions (fixed in v0.9, force-reload required)
• Check-in data resets on page reload until Firebase connected
• Photos lost on reload until Firebase Storage connected
• Flight status is static until FlightAware key added
• Ratings are static until Google Places key added
• FAA drone restriction note for Andrew is advisory only 😄
Commercial Notes
This trip serves as v0.9 live beta. Key West July 2026 is the test case.
Commercial model: $15–20 per trip, up to 20 users, 30-day window.
Target market: multigenerational family trips, friend group vacations, golf weekends, bachelorette trips.
Killer features to highlight: shared photos, I Need buttons, tappable directions, funny help screen.
Next milestone: v1.0 with Firebase live + hosted URL.
Built By
